news_items.title: England vs DR Congo Preview, Lineups & Team News | RotoWire
news_items.text:
England vs DR Congo Preview, Lineups & Team News | RotoWire

# England vs DR Congo Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Round of 32

England vs DR Congo clash in Atlanta for their 2026 World Cup Round of 32 knockout. Predicted lineups, team news, tactical breakdown and score prediction.

June 30, 2026

Share

By:

Pierre Courtin

Adam Zdroik

June 30, 2026

World Cup

### Sign up for free to get the best advice and offers to dominate this season.

Subscribe Log In

From the latest news to creating the best starting lineup, we've got you covered - Sign-up today

# England vs DR Congo Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Round of 32

England open the knockouts as heavy favorites, but this has the look of a grind. They won Group L playing their best football in flashes and their most frustrating football when they sat back, and now they meet a DR Congo side that is built precisely to make life difficult at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta: a resolute low block, a real threat on the counter and a goalkeeper in form.

The danger for England is obvious if you watched the group stage. They were excellent attacking

Story by

England vs DR Congo: World Cup 2026 prediction, kick-off time, TV, live stream, team news, h2h results, odds

England face DR Congo in the World Cup 2026 round of 32 in Atlanta on Wednesday.

It is a first-ever meeting between the two nations, with either co-hosts Mexico or Ecuador lying in wait in the last 16 at Mexico City’s Estadio Azteca on Sunday night.

Advertisement

Advertisement

England start as strong favourites to progress through the first knockout phase, having finished top of Group L after breaking down stubborn Panama in the second half of a 2-0 victory in rainy New Jersey last weekend.

It was an initially laboured but ultimately improved display from the Three Lions, who had sparked concerns among fans after a turgid stalemate with Ghana that had sapped much of the enthusiasm generated by a thrilling 4-2 opening victory over Croatia.

They wi

news_items.title: Thomas Tuchel hit by fresh England injury blow ahead of World Cup knockout clash vs DR Congo
news_items.author:
Charlie Wyett 
			 

			 , Chief football correspondent
news_items.text:
Thomas Tuchel hit by fresh England injury blow ahead of World Cup knockout clash vs DR Congo

Comment now

JARELL QUANSAH looks increasingly likely to miss England’s last 32 clash against DR Congo tomorrow.

Yet crocked Reece James is hoping he could be fit for a last 16 clash in Mexico City.

Your info will be used in accordance with our Privacy Policy

You'll now receive top stories, breaking news, and more, straight to your email.

Stand-in England right-back Jarell Quansah is looking likely to miss England’s clash with DR Congo Credit: Alamy

However, more positively, Reece James could be back for the last 16 if England advance Credit: Getty

England had not given up on Bayer Leverkusen defender Quansah being fit for the clash in Atlanta after he suffered an ankle injury against Panama.

Even though the injury does not look as serious as feared, it seems he will not be fit enough.

So this means that Djed Spence, a surprise choice to even make the 26-man squad after a poor season with Spurs, could start against DR Congo at right-back.

He played at left-back in the second group game against Ghana while he replaced Quansah after he suffered the injury during the 2-0 win over Pan

England open the knockouts as heavy favorites, but this has the look of a grind. They won Group L playing their best football in flashes and their most frustrating football when they sat back, and now they meet a DR Congo side that is built precisely to make life difficult at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta: a resolute low block, a real threat on the counter and a goalkeeper in form.

England vs DR Congo: World Cup 2026 prediction, kick-off time, TV, live stream, team news, h2h results, odds

England face DR Congo in the World Cup 2026 round of 32 in Atlanta on Wednesday.

It is a first-ever meeting between the two nations, with either co-hosts Mexico or Ecuador lying in wait in the last 16 at Mexico City’s Estadio Azteca on Sunday night.

Advertisement

Advertisement

England start as strong favourites to progress through the first knockout phase, having finished top of Group L after breaking down stubborn Panama in the second half of a 2-0 victory in rainy New Jersey last weekend.

It was an initially laboured but ultimately improved display from the Three Lions, who had sparked concerns among fans after a turgid stalemate with Ghana that had sapped much of the enthusiasm generated by a thrilling 4-2 opening victory over Croatia.

They wi"}

JARELL QUANSAH looks increasingly likely to miss England’s last 32 clash against DR Congo tomorrow.

Yet crocked Reece James is hoping he could be fit for a last 16 clash in Mexico City.

Stand-in England right-back Jarell Quansah is looking likely to miss England’s clash with DR Congo Credit: Alamy

However, more positively, Reece James could be back for the last 16 if England advance Credit: Getty

England had not given up on Bayer Leverkusen defender Quansah being fit for the clash in Atlanta after he suffered an ankle injury against Panama.

Even though the injury does not look as serious as feared, it seems he will not be fit enough.

So this means that Djed Spence, a surprise choice to even make the 26-man squad after a poor season with Spurs, could start against DR Congo at right-back.

He played at left-back in the second group game against Ghana while he replaced Quansah after he suffered the injury during the 2-0 win over Pan"}
